title: Israel unites against the 'existential threat' of Iran: 'We need to get rid of this regime that hates us'
subhed: With schools, non-food shopping centers and restaurants closed, Israelis have been living since Saturday under air raid alerts and Iranian missile strikes. These attacks have already claimed several lives.

title: Europeans ready for defensive military actions in region after Iranian strikes in the Gulf
subhed: French President Emmanuel Macron, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said they were 'appalled by the indiscriminate and disproportionate missile attacks' launched by Iran in response to the US-Israeli offensive.
byline: By Elsa Conesa (Berlin (Germany) correspondent), Chloé Hoorman and Philippe Jacqué (Brussels, Europe bureau)

title: What we know about the US-Israeli operation that killed Ali Khamenei
subhed: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in a major attack by Israel and the United States over the weekend.

title: French nuclear 'umbrella' for Europe now increasingly discussed among allies
subhed: French President Emmanuel Macron will deliver a speech on nuclear deterrence on Monday, with Europeans expected to pay close attention amid threats from Russia and growing doubts over the United States' reliability as a NATO ally.
byline: By Hélène Bienvenu (Warsaw (Poland) correspondent), Elsa Conesa (Berlin (Germany) correspondent), Philippe Ricard and Elise Vincent

title: Trump's war of trial and error in Iran
subhed: The president has made numerous, often confusing media appearances since the start of the offensive on February 28, while only a third of Americans believe that the US-Israeli airstrikes are justified.

subhed: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'

title: France-Germany-UK's balancing act in response to US-Israeli offensive against Iran
subhed: In a joint statement indicating a form of tacit support without offensive participation, France, Germany and the United Kingdom stressed that they had not participated in the strikes against Tehran and criticized the Iranian attacks against countries in the region.

title: Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader and symbol of a repressive regime, has died
subhed: The successor to Ayatollah Khomeini in 1989, Ali Khamenei embodied the Islamic Republic, setting its political course both domestically and abroad. Over the course of his rule, and faced with the country's isolation, he chose to harden the regime and brutally repress any dissent. He died on Saturday, February 28, at the age of 86.
